seoTitle: How to Create a Confluence Personal Space (2026)
metaDescription: Set up a private Confluence space for drafts and personal notes, keeping unfinished work out of shared company directories until it's ready.
intro: Rough drafts and unfinished ideas don't belong in a shared company directory before they're ready. This guide covers creating a private personal space in Confluence, keeping it locked to yourself by default, and organizing your own notes and task lists inside it.

steps.heading: Open your profile menu
steps.body: Move to the top right corner of your canvas and click your profile avatar icon.

steps.heading: Add a personal space
steps.body: Select the 'Add Personal Space' option from the expanded dropdown menu.

steps.heading: Name and set your URL
steps.body: Type a search-optimized space title and edit the default URL subdomain handle.

steps.heading: Create your homepage
steps.body: Click the Create button to instantly build your private homepage and sidebar directory.

steps.heading: Verify your privacy settings
steps.body: Click the lock permissions icon to confirm external users are excluded from your space by default.

steps.heading: Build out your notes
steps.body: Use inline slash commands to build personal goal boards, to-do lists, and draft documents, then publish to save your structure.

faq.question: Is a Confluence personal space private by default?
faq.answer: Yes, it's meant to be locked to 'Me Only' by default, and you should keep that restriction unless you intentionally choose to share specific pages.

faq.question: Does a personal space use up my company's group license?
faq.answer: No, personal spaces are tied to your individual account and don't consume company group licenses.

faq.question: Can I share a page from my personal space later?
faq.answer: Yes, once a draft is ready, you can adjust its permission settings to share it with specific stakeholders.

faq.question: Can I organize multiple notes inside my personal space?
faq.answer: Yes, you can drag and rearrange your private notes into a nested parent-child sidebar structure to keep things scannable.
